key: Falcon 1 launch history key milestones first flight first success retirement|5

value.title: Falcon 1 - Wikipedia
value.content: # Falcon 1. | Falcon 1 flight 4, the first successful launch of a privately developed, fully liquid-fueled rocket to enter orbit around the Earth. **Falcon 1** was a two-stage small-lift launch vehicle that was operated from 2006 to 2009 by SpaceX, an American aerospace manufacturer. The Falcon 1 used LOX/RP-1 for both stages, the first stage powered by a single pump-fed Merlin engine, and the second stage powered by SpaceX's pressure-fed Kestrel vacuum engine. On July 14, 2009, Falcon 1 made its second successful flight, delivering the Malaysian RazakSAT satellite to orbit on SpaceX's first commercial launch (fifth and final launch overall). While SpaceX had announced an enhanced variant, the Falcon 1e, following this flight, the Falcon 1 was retired in favor of the Falcon 9 v1.0, the first version of the company’s successful and long-running Falcon 9 launch vehicle. While the development of Falcon 1 was privately funded, the first two Falcon 1 launches were purchased by the United States Department of Defense under a program that evaluates new US launch vehicles suitable for use by DARPA.

value.title: Falcon 1 — Grokipedia
value.content: Powered by a Merlin engine on the first stage using RP-1 (refined kerosene) and liquid oxygen (LOX) propellants, and a Kestrel engine on the second stage, it stood approximately 21.3 m (70 ft) tall with a diameter of 1.7 m (5.5 ft). Launched from Omelek Island in the Kwajalein Atoll, the rocket conducted five flights between 2006 and 2009, achieving two successful orbital insertions and marking a pivotal milestone as the first privately developed liquid-fueled rocket to reach orbit.](#ref-1)](#ref-2)](#ref-3) Developed by SpaceX—founded in 2002 by Elon Musk with the goal of reducing space access costs—the Falcon 1 represented the company's inaugural launch vehicle, emphasizing reusability in design principles despite being expendable in operation. The fourth flight on September 28, 2008, succeeded in deploying a dummy payload called "RatSat," validating the vehicle's reliability and earning SpaceX a $1.6 billion Commercial Resupply Services contract from NASA shortly thereafter.](#ref-4)](#ref-5)](#ref-6) The fifth and final launch on July 14, 2009, carried the Malaysian RazakSAT Earth-observation satellite (approximately 180 kg) into a 685 km near-equatorial orbit, marking SpaceX's first commercial mission.

value.title: Falcon 1 | Golden
value.content: On 14 July 2009, Falcon 1 made its second and final flight, successfully delivering the Malaysian satellite to orbit on SpaceX's first commercial launch (fifth launch overall). Following this flight, the Falcon 1 was retired and succeeded by Falcon 9.

value.title: Falcon 1 | Starship SpaceX Wiki | Fandom
value.content: March 18, 2026 - The launch went successfully and SECO occurred at T+9:35. The second stage engine then relit at T+52:29 to circularize the final orbit for the payload.[7] Falcon 1 was retired after this flight .

value.title: r/spacex on Reddit: 15 years ago today, Falcon 1 was the first privately developed liquid fuel rocket to reach Earth orbit
value.content: September 28, 2023 - 10 years ago today: SpaceX launches Falcon 1 from the Kwajalein Atoll, the company's first rocket to successfully reach orbit . ... If this fourth flight of Falcon 1 failed 9+ years ago, there would be no SpaceX today.
